actionable_feedback: {'id': 'af_1', 'title': 'Embed an explicit manufacturability rubric sheet', 'detail': "Add a dedicated sheet (e.g., 'Manufacturability_Rubric') that documents the subjective scoring rules, scales (e.g., 1-5), definitions for each rubric criterion, and example subjective scores for Options A/B/C. If a score is unknown, keep the 'needs_review' marker but include a recommended method and temporary stand-in inputs so graders understand how to convert expert judgments into numeric entries.", 'priority': 'high', 'verifiable_by': 'Open artifact and confirm the new sheet exists and contains rubric definitions and example cells referencing Option rows.'}

{'id': 'af_2', 'title': 'Populate or document manufacturability entries (or clearly flag and explain)', 'detail': "Where manufacturability values are unavailable, retain 'needs_review' but add explanatory comments/cells describing why missing and how to obtain them. Provide a provisional approach (e.g., default conservative score or procedure to gather expert input) and mark any provisional values as 'subjective' in the sheet.", 'priority': 'high', 'verifiable_by': "Check that each Option has either a numeric manufacturability score or an explicit 'needs_review' cell with adjacent justification text/cell references."}

{'id': 'af_3', 'title': 'Add explicit cell citations in Decision sheet for every recommendation', 'detail': "In the Decision sheet, for each recommendation include explicit formula references (e.g., =Calc!G12) or text cells that list the exact source cells used for the final numeric ranking and for any 'needs_review' decisions. Also include cached numeric values beside formula cells so graders can read final numbers even if formula parsing fails.", 'priority': 'medium', 'verifiable_by': 'Open Decision sheet and confirm explicit source-cell citations are present and that cached numeric values are visible.'}

{'id': 'af_4', 'title': 'Provide a parser-friendly supplementary export', 'detail': "Export the key sheets (Data, Calc, Decision, Manufacturability_Rubric) as CSV or JSON alongside the .xlsx and include a small plaintext 'verifier_summary.txt' that lists the final computed scores, the provisional leader, missing inputs, and exact cell citations. This mitigates environment parsing problems (e.g., openpyxl missing).", 'priority': 'medium', 'verifiable_by': 'Check that CSV/JSON exports and verifier_summary.txt are present in the attempt outputs and match values shown in the workbook.'}

{'id': 'af_5', 'title': 'Document handling of missing inputs in checks and sensitivity', 'detail': "In Checks and Sensitivity sheets explicitly show how the final score is computed when one or more components are missing (e.g., compute 'known-score subtotal' and show sensitivity bounds). Add a short note in Checks that points to the cells used for the documented sensitivity analysis so a verifier can reproduce reasoning without full formula parsing.", 'priority': 'medium', 'verifiable_by': 'Confirm Checks sheet contains a documented known-score subtotal calculation and explicit links to sensitivity scenarios with cell references.'}
artifact_specific_comments: The artifact appears to contain the expected sheets (Decision, Data, Assumptions, Calc, Sensitivity, Checks) and preserved raw packet values. The primary weakness is missing manufacturability numeric entries and lack of an embedded manufacturability rubric.
feedback_summary: Baseline submission produced the required fracture_comparison.xlsx with correctly separated Data/Calc/Decision/Sensitivity/Checks sheets and preserved raw packet values. The grader deducted a small amount because manufacturability inputs were missing (flagged as 'needs_review') and because the verifier environment could not parse the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable), which reduced ability to confirm formulas and explicit cell citations. Overall deliverable presence and structure pass, but verifiability and the embedded subjective scoring rubric need improvement.

verifier_notes.artifact_preview_parse_failed: No module named 'openpyxl'; grader relied on metadata and trace rather than cell-level verification.
verifier_notes.scoring_rationale: Small deduction for missing manufacturability rubric and inability to fully parse workbook for explicit cell citations.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model evaluator generated actionable feedback and revision plan.

actionable_feedback.detail: Add a dedicated sheet (e.g., 'Manufacturability_Rubric') that documents the subjective scoring rules, scales (e.g., 1-5), definitions for each rubric criterion, and example subjective scores for Options A/B/C. If a score is unknown, keep the 'needs_review' marker but include a recommended method and temporary stand-in inputs so graders understand how to convert expert judgments into numeric entries.
actionable_feedback.title: Embed an explicit manufacturability rubric sheet
actionable_feedback.verifiable_by: Open artifact and confirm the new sheet exists and contains rubric definitions and example cells referencing Option rows.

actionable_feedback.detail: Where manufacturability values are unavailable, retain 'needs_review' but add explanatory comments/cells describing why missing and how to obtain them. Provide a provisional approach (e.g., default conservative score or procedure to gather expert input) and mark any provisional values as 'subjective' in the sheet.
actionable_feedback.title: Populate or document manufacturability entries (or clearly flag and explain)
actionable_feedback.verifiable_by: Check that each Option has either a numeric manufacturability score or an explicit 'needs_review' cell with adjacent justification text/cell references.

actionable_feedback.detail: In the Decision sheet, for each recommendation include explicit formula references (e.g., =Calc!G12) or text cells that list the exact source cells used for the final numeric ranking and for any 'needs_review' decisions. Also include cached numeric values beside formula cells so graders can read final numbers even if formula parsing fails.
actionable_feedback.title: Add explicit cell citations in Decision sheet for every recommendation
actionable_feedback.verifiable_by: Open Decision sheet and confirm explicit source-cell citations are present and that cached numeric values are visible.

actionable_feedback.detail: Export the key sheets (Data, Calc, Decision, Manufacturability_Rubric) as CSV or JSON alongside the .xlsx and include a small plaintext 'verifier_summary.txt' that lists the final computed scores, the provisional leader, missing inputs, and exact cell citations. This mitigates environment parsing problems (e.g., openpyxl missing).
actionable_feedback.title: Provide a parser-friendly supplementary export
actionable_feedback.verifiable_by: Check that CSV/JSON exports and verifier_summary.txt are present in the attempt outputs and match values shown in the workbook.

actionable_feedback.detail: In Checks and Sensitivity sheets explicitly show how the final score is computed when one or more components are missing (e.g., compute 'known-score subtotal' and show sensitivity bounds). Add a short note in Checks that points to the cells used for the documented sensitivity analysis so a verifier can reproduce reasoning without full formula parsing.
actionable_feedback.title: Document handling of missing inputs in checks and sensitivity
actionable_feedback.verifiable_by: Confirm Checks sheet contains a documented known-score subtotal calculation and explicit links to sensitivity scenarios with cell references.

artifact_specific_comments: The artifact appears to contain the expected sheets (Decision, Data, Assumptions, Calc, Sensitivity, Checks) and preserved raw packet values. The primary weakness is missing manufacturability numeric entries and lack of an embedded manufacturability rubric.
raw_llm_output.feedback_summary: Baseline submission produced the required fracture_comparison.xlsx with correctly separated Data/Calc/Decision/Sensitivity/Checks sheets and preserved raw packet values. The grader deducted a small amount because manufacturability inputs were missing (flagged as 'needs_review') and because the verifier environment could not parse the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable), which reduced ability to confirm formulas and explicit cell citations. Overall deliverable presence and structure pass, but verifiability and the embedded subjective scoring rubric need improvement.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Add a Manufacturability_Rubric sheet with documented subjective scoring rules and example scores, ensure Decision sheet contains explicit cell citations and cached result values, and attach parser-friendly exports (CSV/JSON and a verifier_summary.txt) so verifiers can fully validate formulas and final recommendations even if openpyxl is unavailable.
trace_specific_comments: Trace shows a manual OOXML fallback authoring approach and explicit notes that 'manufacturability' values are missing and flagged as 'needs_review'. The runtime also attempted to use an artifact library which was unavailable; include a parser-friendly export to mitigate grader environment limits.
